global_allocator_candidate_pool_asymmetr: pure-S enumerates OBS-refit supports at every budget-band extremum and declared fraction; QSL enumerates rank-conditioned supports, so only the byte oracle, cap, support scorer and OBS value refit are shared

pure-L independently fits the unchanged Q residual at each rank and reuses the original local Q+L factor at its rank; QSL fits each rank after a rank-conditioned sparse support, so residual targets differ even though the factorizer settings, byte oracle and aggregate cap are shared

this no-joint control uses exactly the Q, pure-S/OBS and pure-L states nested in the QSL candidate pool; QSL differs only by additionally admitting same-layer joint S+L states and its audited local sentinel
